headlines.title: GERMAN ENTRY INTO BRUSSELS IN WAR GRAY
headlines.subtitle: Uniforms, Helmets, Guns and Pontoons All of the Same Color.
headlines.fullText:
Bu Associated Press.
LONDON. Aug. 12 -- A dispatch to the Daily Mail from Brussels by way of Ghent adds the following details concerning the entry of the Germans Into Brussels:
"Burgomaster Max met the Ger- mans outside the city. He endeavored to asert the claims of the populace to certain rights under the rules of war. waterrupted by 'nin German Officers, who fristed that he remove the scarf of office. gauMont beggecoupled and the civil attairs of the end ) time he was warned that he would he held responsible personally for any overt acts.

Hamm did not hear the warning. but. just like the others, he stepped into deep water and was carried into the suck hole. He ran called to the for bank help and Jones Prince stretched out fishing poles for him to in the whirl, and he went under. He did not come to the surface again.
